annotations.label: Euler angles

anchor.snippet: The Euler angles are three angles

annotations.note: No Euler-angle-specific declaration matching this definition was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Euler angles for frames

anchor.snippet: They can also represent the orientation of a mobile frame

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes orthonormal bases as frames, but I found no declaration representing frame orientation by Euler angles.

annotations.label: Classic vertical zero convention

anchor.snippet: zero degrees represent the vertical orientation

annotations.note: No declaration for this Euler-angle zero convention was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Alternative horizontal zero convention

anchor.snippet: zero degrees represent the horizontal position

annotations.note: No declaration for this alternative Euler-angle zero convention was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Definitions by geometry or rotations

anchor.snippet: Euler angles can be defined by elemental geometry or by composition of rotations

annotations.note: Mathlib has special orthogonal matrices as rotation infrastructure, but no equivalence between geometric and chained-rotation Euler-angle definitions was found.

annotations.label: Three rotations suffice

anchor.snippet: three consecutive elemental rotations

annotations.note: The rotation group infrastructure exists, but I found no Euler decomposition theorem saying three elemental rotations suffice.

annotations.label: Extrinsic rotations

anchor.snippet: extrinsic (rotations about the axes xyz of the original coordinate system

annotations.note: No declaration for extrinsic Euler rotations about fixed axes was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Intrinsic rotations

anchor.snippet: intrinsic (rotations about the axes of the rotating coordinate system XYZ

annotations.note: No declaration for intrinsic Euler rotations about moving axes was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Primed axis notation

anchor.snippet: axis designation with a prime mark superscript

annotations.note: No declaration for primed-axis notation in Euler-angle conventions was found in Mathlib/.

anchor.snippet: Euler angles are typically denoted as

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration for the conventional Euler-angle symbols was found.

anchor.snippet: there exist twelve possible sequences of rotation axes

anchor.snippet: Tait-Bryan angles are also called Cardan angles

annotations.label: Broad Euler angle terminology

anchor.snippet: both kinds of sequences are called "Euler angles"

annotations.note: No declaration formalizing this terminology convention was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Classic Euler angles

anchor.snippet: The Euler angles are three angles introduced by Swiss mathematician Leonhard Euler

annotations.note: No declaration for classical Euler angles or their historical definition was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Line of nodes

anchor.snippet: begins by defining the line of nodes (N) as the intersection of the planes xy and XY

annotations.note: No declaration for the Euler-angle line of nodes was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Line of nodes as common perpendicular

anchor.snippet: it can also be defined as the common perpendicular to the axes z and Z

annotations.label: Geometric Euler angles

anchor.snippet: the three Euler angles can be defined as follows

annotations.note: No declaration for the three geometric Euler-angle definitions was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: First geometric Euler angle

anchor.snippet: is the signed angle between the x axis and the N axis

annotations.note: No declaration for this first geometric Euler angle was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Second geometric Euler angle

anchor.snippet: is the angle between the z axis and the Z axis

annotations.note: No declaration for this second geometric Euler angle was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Third geometric Euler angle

anchor.snippet: is the signed angle between the N axis and the X axis

annotations.note: No declaration for this third geometric Euler angle was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Same-handed frames condition

anchor.snippet: Euler angles between two reference frames are defined only if both frames have the same handedness

annotations.note: Mathlib proves determinant-one change-of-basis for same-orientation orthonormal bases, but it does not define Euler angles only for same-handed frames.

annotations.label: Intrinsic rotations

anchor.snippet: Intrinsic rotations are elemental rotations that occur about the axes of a coordinate system XYZ attached to a moving body

annotations.note: No declaration for body-attached intrinsic Euler rotations was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Intrinsic axes change orientation

anchor.snippet: they change their orientation after each elemental rotation

annotations.note: No declaration describing changing intrinsic axes after each Euler rotation was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Intrinsic rotations reach any orientation

anchor.snippet: a composition of three intrinsic rotations can be used to reach any target orientation for XYZ

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the special orthogonal group, but no theorem for reachability by three intrinsic rotations was found.

annotations.label: Euler angles by intrinsic rotations

anchor.snippet: Euler angles can be defined by intrinsic rotations

annotations.note: No declaration defining Euler angles by intrinsic rotations was found in Mathlib/.

annotations.label: Intrinsic line of nodes

annotations.label: Node as primed x-axis

anchor.snippet: N can be simply denoted x ′

annotations.label: Third rotation preserves Z orientation

anchor.snippet: the third elemental rotation occurs about Z

annotations.label: Final Z equals double-primed z

anchor.snippet: Z coincides with z ″

annotations.label: Euler angles as rotation amplitudes

anchor.snippet: are the amplitudes of these elemental rotations

anchor.snippet: The XYZ system rotates again, but this time about the x axis by β

anchor.snippet: The XYZ system rotates a third time, about the z axis again, by angle α

annotations.label: ZXZ sequence notation

annotations.label: Intrinsic 3-1-3 notation

anchor.snippet: Intrinsic rotation can also be denoted 3-1-3

annotations.label: Right-hand angle convention

anchor.snippet: Angles are commonly defined according to the right-hand rule

annotations.label: Positive angle sign

annotations.label: Negative angle sign

anchor.snippet: negative values when the rotation appears counter-clockwise

annotations.label: Alpha and gamma range

anchor.snippet: the range is defined modulo 2 π radians

annotations.label: Beta range

annotations.label: Beta valid ranges

anchor.snippet: it could be [0, π ] or [− π /2, π /2]
